detailed_explanation: Period between two points in time or space. Used to indicate duration or spatial gap.
example_sentences: ["授業の間 - Between classes","１時間の間 - For one hour","東京と大阪の間 - Between Tokyo and Osaka"]
usage_notes: Time: 〜の間に (during), 〜の間 (throughout). Space: AとBの間 (between A and B).

detailed_explanation: To ascend, rise, or enter someone's house. Intransitive verb with multiple uses.
example_sentences: ["温度が上がる - Temperature rises","家に上がる - Enter a house","値段が上がった - Price went up"]
usage_notes: Different from 上げる (transitive). Can mean 'enter house' as polite expression.

detailed_explanation: Various places, scattered locations. Indicates movement to multiple places.
example_sentences: ["あちこちに行く - Go here and there","あちこちで会った - Met in various places","あちこちを探す - Search all over"]
usage_notes: Similar to いろいろなところ. Used for traveling, searching, or scattered distribution.

detailed_explanation: People or things coming together naturally. Intransitive - no direct object.
example_sentences: ["みんなが集まる - Everyone gathers","集まってください - Please gather","集まりますか - Will you gather?"]
usage_notes: Intransitive. For deliberate collection use 集める (transitive).

detailed_explanation: Actively collecting or gathering something. Transitive - takes direct object.
example_sentences: ["切手を集める - Collect stamps","みんなを集める - Gather everyone","お金を集める - Collect money"]
usage_notes: Transitive. Contrast: 集まる (intransitive gathering).

detailed_explanation: To express apology or regret. Important in Japanese social harmony.
example_sentences: ["先生に謝る - Apologize to teacher","謝ります - I apologize","謝らなければ - Must apologize"]
usage_notes: 謝り = apology noun form. 謝る文化 important in Japan. Level: 申し訳ありません (formal) < 謝みません < 謝る.

detailed_explanation: To wash things, body parts, or clothes. Common daily activity.
example_sentences: ["手を洗う - Wash hands","顔を洗う - Wash face","車を洗う - Wash car"]
usage_notes: 手を洗う very common (hygiene). 洗濯する for laundry/clothes specifically.

detailed_explanation: To walk, travel on foot. Different from 歩行 which is more formal/noun.
example_sentences: ["学校まで歩く - Walk to school","毎日歩く - Walk every day","公園を歩く - Walk in park"]
usage_notes: 〜まで歩く (walk to), 〜を歩く (walk through). Common exercise in Japan.

detailed_explanation: Greater than or equal to; also used to end presentations ('that's all').
example_sentences: ["18歳以上 - 18 years or older","以上です - That's all (end of speech)","予想以上 - Above expectations"]
usage_notes: Includes the number (X以上 = X and above). Common in rules, age limits.

detailed_explanation: To hurry, rush, do quickly. Used when time is limited.
example_sentences: ["急いで行く - Go in a hurry","急がないと - If I don't hurry","急いでください - Please hurry"]
usage_notes: 急いで = quickly. Common phrase: 急がば回れ (more haste less speed).

detailed_explanation: Humble form of する. Used when speaking about own actions toward superiors.
example_sentences: ["お手伝いいたします - I will help","案内いたします - I will guide","お持ちいたします - I will hold/carry"]
usage_notes: 謙譲語 (humble language). Never used for others' actions. Makes speaker lower.

detailed_explanation: Doing something with someone; being in same state/place.
example_sentences: ["一緒に行く - Go together","一緒です - We're together/same","いっしょに食べる - Eat together"]
usage_notes: 一緒に = together. Very common in invitations. 〜と一緒 (with 〜).

detailed_explanation: The first, best, or most. Used for superlatives.
example_sentences: ["一番好き - Like the most","一番大きい - The biggest","日本で一番 - Number one in Japan"]
usage_notes: 〜で一番 = most in ~. Can be followed by adjective (一番高い) or noun (一番の友達).

detailed_explanation: To be gone, disappear, pass away (euphemism). Combination of いない + なる.
example_sentences: ["猫が居なくなった - Cat disappeared","居なくならないで - Don't go away","お金が居なくなる - Money runs out"]
usage_notes: Can mean 'die' (polite euphemism). Also for things running out/disappearing.

detailed_explanation: To put something inside something. Transitive (inserts something).
example_sentences: ["ゴミを入れる - Put in trash","お金を入れる - Insert money","入れてください - Please put in"]
usage_notes: Transitive. Intransitive: 入る (はいる). Common: 入れ物 (container).

detailed_explanation: Physical position above; also 'after doing X' (〜た上で).
example_sentences: ["上に置く - Put on top","考えた上で - After thinking","上の階 - Upper floor"]
usage_notes: 〜の上 = on top of. 〜た上で = after doing. 上に = furthermore (transition word).

detailed_explanation: Position behind something/someone. Opposite of 前 (front).
example_sentences: ["後ろにいる - Behind","後ろを向く - Turn back","後ろ姿 - Figure from behind"]
usage_notes: 後ろに (behind). 後ろから (from behind). 後ろ髪を引かれる = reluctant to leave.

detailed_explanation: A lie, untruth. Opposite of 本当 (truth).
example_sentences: ["嘘をつく - Tell a lie","嘘でしょ - That's a lie, right?","嘘です - It's a lie"]
usage_notes: 嘘をつく = tell a lie. 嘘つき = liar. 嘘八百 = pack of lies.

detailed_explanation: Indicating thanks or (sarcastic) blame. お陰で = thanks to.
example_sentences: ["あなたのお陰で - Thanks to you","お陰で晴れた - Thanks to you it cleared up","お陰さまで元気 - Thanks to you I'm fine"]
usage_notes: Usually positive: お陰さまで. Can be sarcastic: あいつのお陰で遅れた (because of him...).

detailed_explanation: To place something somewhere; to leave something. Transitive.
example_sentences: ["机に置く - Put on desk","置いてある - Is placed","置いていく - Leave behind"]
usage_notes: 置いておく = leave for later. 置いてある = has been placed (state).

detailed_explanation: To become angry or to scold someone. Intransitive and transitive uses.
example_sentences: ["先生が怒る - Teacher gets angry","怒らないで - Don't be angry","怒っている - Is angry"]
usage_notes: 〜に怒られる = get scolded by 〜. 怒っている = angry (state).

detailed_explanation: Regrettable waste; precious; close but not quite (almost had it).
example_sentences: ["惜しい - What a waste/So close","惜しく思う - Feel it's a waste","あと少しで惜しい - So close, almost had it"]
usage_notes: 惜しい = close but missed (sports, exams). 後 slightly would have succeeded.

detailed_explanation: To teach, inform, or show. Used for knowledge transfer.
example_sentences: ["日本語を教える - Teach Japanese","道を教える - Tell the way","教えてください - Please tell me"]
usage_notes: 教えて = tell me (very common). 教える人 = teacher/instructor.

detailed_explanation: To drop something (transitive). To lose/omit something.
example_sentences: ["財布を落とした - Dropped wallet","コップを落とす - Drop a cup","落下として落とす - Drop down"]
usage_notes: Transitive (you drop something). Intransitive: 落ちる (falls by itself).

detailed_explanation: To fall down, drop by itself. Intransitive - happens naturally.
example_sentences: ["木から落ちる - Fall from tree","落ちた - Fell","落ちないで - Don't fall"]
usage_notes: Intransitive. Contrast: 落とす (transitive - you drop). 落ち着く = calm down.

detailed_explanation: To commit to memory, learn by heart. Includes intentional learning.
example_sentences: ["単語を覚える - Memorize vocabulary","覚えてない - Don't remember","覚えた - Remembered"]
usage_notes: 覚える = active memorization. 覚えている = know/remember (state).

detailed_explanation: Physical weight or serious/important matters. Opposite of 軽い (light).
example_sentences: ["重い荷物 - Heavy luggage","重い病気 - Serious illness","気が重い - Feel heavy (depressed)"]
usage_notes: 物理的: heavy weight. 抽象的: serious/responsible. 気が重い = feeling down.

detailed_explanation: Front/obverse side; official/public position. Opposite of 裏 (back).
example_sentences: ["表に書く - Write on front","表と裏 - Front and back","表の顔 - Public face"]
usage_notes: 表 = front/official. 表向き = ostensibly/officially. 表す = express/display.

detailed_explanation: To get off/down from (train, mountain, etc.). Intransitive.
example_sentences: ["電車を下りる - Get off train","山を下りる - Descend mountain","下りてください - Please get off"]
usage_notes: Train: 電車を下りる. 降りる also used for alighting. Contrast: 上る (ascend).

detailed_explanation: To exchange, switch, or replace. Money, seats, etc.
example_sentences: ["お金を換える - Exchange money","席を換える - Change seats","換えてください - Please exchange"]
usage_notes: Same pronunciation: 換える (exchange), 替える (replace), 代える (substitute).

detailed_explanation: Multiple meanings: cost, hang, depend, catch. Intransitive.
example_sentences: ["時間が掛かる - Takes time","3時間掛かる - Takes 3 hours","気を掛ける - Worry about","病気に掛る - Fall ill"]
usage_notes: 〜に掛かる = takes (time/money). 気が掛かる = worried. Very versatile verb.

detailed_explanation: To write characters, letters, documents. Basic communication verb.
example_sentences: ["字を書く - Write characters","手紙を書く - Write a letter","書いてください - Please write"]
usage_notes: 書く for handwriting. 打つ for typing. 書き方 = way of writing.

detailed_explanation: Extremely versatile: hang up, make call, multiply (math), sit (put on).
example_sentences: ["電話を掛ける - Make a phone call","絵を掛ける - Hang a picture","１に１を掛ける - Multiply 2 by 2","腰を掛ける - Sit down"]
usage_notes: Most common: 電話を掛ける. Very high frequency verb with many meanings.

detailed_explanation: Common cold, influenza. Cultural note: big concern in Japan.
example_sentences: ["風邪を引く - Catch a cold","風邪が治る - Cold gets better","風邪をひいた - Caught a cold"]
usage_notes: 風邪を引く = catch cold. Very common in Japan. Masks worn when having 風邪.

detailed_explanation: Way of doing, direction, or polite reference to person. Multiple uses.
example_sentences: ["作り方 - Way of making","この方 - This person (polite)","教え方 - Way of teaching","どちらの方 - Which person"]
usage_notes: 〜方 = way of doing. 方 as person = polite alternative to 人.

detailed_explanation: Win, victory, success. Opposite of 負け (defeat).
example_sentences: ["勝ちです - It's a win","勝ち負け - Win or lose","勝ちを目指す - Aim for victory"]
usage_notes: 勝ち = noun form of winning. 勝つ = verb. 勝ち組 = winner (socially).

detailed_explanation: To win, defeat, be victorious. Opposite of 負ける (lose).
example_sentences: ["試合に勝つ - Win a match","勝ちたい - Want to win","勝てる - Can win"]
usage_notes: 〜に勝つ = win against. 勝てる = potential form (can win). Important in sports/competition culture.

detailed_explanation: Sad, sorrowful, unfortunate. Emotional state of sadness.
example_sentences: ["悲しい知らせ - Sad news","悲しくて泣く - Cry from sadness","悲しいですね - That's sad","悲しむ - Grieve"]
usage_notes: 悲しい news, events, situations. 悲しむ = verb form (grieve). 悲しみ = sadness.

detailed_explanation: To wear on head (hats, helmets), or to suffer/undertake (damage, expense).
example_sentences: ["帽子を被る - Wear a hat","怪我を被る - Suffer an injury","損を被る - Suffer a loss"]
usage_notes: Headwear only (different from 着る/履く/める). Also: suffer consequences = 被害を被る.

detailed_explanation: River, stream, large natural water flow. Important geographical feature.
example_sentences: ["川を渡る - Cross a river","川で遊ぶ - Play in river","大きな川 - Big river","川沿い - Along the river"]
usage_notes: Many place names with 川. 川遊び = river play (summer activity). 河 also means river (more formal).
